findings.name: CAA Record
findings.description: A CAA record was discovered. A CAA record is used to specify which certificate authorities (CAs) are allowed to issue certificates for a domain.
findings.request:
;; opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 38630
;; flags: rd; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 1

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version 0; flags:; udp: 4096

;; QUESTION SECTION:
;demo.testfire.net.	IN	 CAA
findings.response:
;; opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 38630
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0, AUTHORITY: 1, ADDITIONAL: 1

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version 0; flags:; udp: 1232

;; QUESTION SECTION:
;demo.testfire.net.	IN	 CAA

;; AUTHORITY SECTION:
testfire.net.	86400	IN	SOA	asia3.akam.net. hostmaster.akamai.com. 1366025607 43200 7200 604800 86400

findings.name: Apache Detection
findings.description: Some Apache servers have the version on the response header. The OpenSSL version can be also obtained

findings.name: Detect SSL Certificate Issuer
findings.description: Extract the issuer's organization from the target's certificate. Issuers are entities which sign and distribute certificates.

findings.name: SSL DNS Names
findings.description: Extract the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) from the target's certificate. SAN facilitates the usage of additional hostnames with the same certificate.

findings.name: Deprecated TLS Detection
findings.description: Both TLS 1.1 and SSLv3 are deprecated in favor of stronger encryption.
findings.remediation: Update the web server's TLS configuration to disable TLS 1.1 and SSLv3.

findings.name: TLS Version - Detect
findings.description:
TLS version detection is a security process used to determine the version of the Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol used by a computer or server.
It is important to detect the TLS version in order to ensure secure communication between two computers or servers.

findings.name: Weak Cipher Suites Detection
findings.description: A weak cipher is defined as an encryption/decryption algorithm that uses a key of insufficient length. Using an insufficient length for a key in an encryption/decryption algorithm opens up the possibility (or probability) that the encryption scheme could be broken.

findings.name: Deprecated TLS Detection
findings.description: Both TLS 1.1 and SSLv3 are deprecated in favor of stronger encryption.
findings.remediation: Update the web server's TLS configuration to disable TLS 1.1 and SSLv3.

findings.name: TLS Version - Detect
findings.description:
TLS version detection is a security process used to determine the version of the Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol used by a computer or server.
It is important to detect the TLS version in order to ensure secure communication between two computers or servers.

findings.name: Weak Cipher Suites Detection
findings.description: A weak cipher is defined as an encryption/decryption algorithm that uses a key of insufficient length. Using an insufficient length for a key in an encryption/decryption algorithm opens up the possibility (or probability) that the encryption scheme could be broken.

It is important to detect the TLS version in order to ensure secure communication between two computers or servers.
